Main shrine building | Location = 373-10 Moribe, Anpachi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture | Coordinates = {{coord|35|20|37.35|N|136|41|5.70|E|scale:20000}} | Deities = [[Amaterasu]], [[Toyouke-hime]], [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami | Shrine Rank = Shikinaisha (Small), GЕҚsha | Established = Unknown }} '''Ubanato Shrine''' (е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ) is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|е…ғдјҠеӢў|lt=Moto-Ise shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} located in Anpachi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}, representing Ubanato Shrine in Anpachi District, Mino Province. Another candidate is {{ill|Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (зҘһжҲёз”ә)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|qq=}}. Under the {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Ranking system|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}, it held the Rank of a GЕҚsha. {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|е…ғдјҠеӢў|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} are [[Shinto shrine]]s that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Jingu]].{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=7pEOicqsjv0C&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA201|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai: Archaeology, History, and Mythology|date=2007-02-28|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3035-9|language=en}}{{Cite book|last=Stalker|first=Nancy K.|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=v44BEAAAQBAJ&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA62|title=Prophet Motive: Deguchi OnisaburЕҚ, Oomoto, and the Rise of New Religions in Imperial Japan|date=2007-10-31|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6404-0|language=en}} The first one was {{ill|ЕҢmiwa Shrine|ja|еӨ§зҘһзҘһзӨҫ|lt=Hibara Shrine|lt_ja=жӘңеҺҹзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢmiwa Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2011-08-10|title=Hibara Jinja and Amaterasu|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2011/08/11/hibara-jinja-and-amaterasu/|access-date=2023-10-18|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}{{Cite web|title=Hibara Jinja ShrineпјҲNaraпјү|url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/en/location/290100006/?search=1&q=Hibara+Shrine|access-date=2023-10-03|website=Nationwide location database|language=en}} ==History== The founding date is unknown. According to tradition, when Princess Yamatohime-no-mikoto was searching for a suitable place to enshrine Amaterasu during the reign of Emperor Suinin, she passed through this site while traveling from {{ill|Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|ja|еӨ©зҘһзҘһзӨҫ (з‘һз©ӮеёӮ)|lt=Ikurakawa-no-miya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|qq=}} in Mino Province to Owari Province. Another theory suggests that this shrine itself was originally Ikurakawa-no-miya, supported by an inscription found on the reverse side of its treasured sacred mirror. The area historically suffered frequent flood damage. The original location of the shrine is unclear, but records indicate relocation occurred in 1603. During the Edo period, the shrine was under the protection of Owari Domain. *In 1924, the shrine relocated due to upstream construction work on the Kiso River. *In 1933, relocated again due to embankment work on the Nagara River. *In 1934, relocated to its current site due to reconstruction work on the Sai River. ==Enshrined Deities== *[[Amaterasu]] *[[Toyouke-hime]] *[[Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] *Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami ==Transportation== *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Gikaki Line toward Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University or Gifu Distribution Center). *Approx. 2.5 km from "Minami-Omori" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Hashima Line toward Gifu-Hashima Station). *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Gifu Bus Obusa-Sunomata Line). *5-minute walk from "Moribe" bus stop (Anpachi Community Bus). ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)|version=77963473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Anpachi, Gifu]] [[Category:History of Anpachi, Gifu]] }} [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]] [[en:draft:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]] [[ja:е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)]]

Main shrine building | Location = 373-10 Moribe, Anpachi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture | Coordinates = {{coord|35|20|37.35|N|136|41|5.70|E|scale:20000}} | Deities = [[Amaterasu]], [[Toyouke-hime]], [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami | Shrine Rank = Shikinaisha (Small), GЕҚsha | Established = Unknown }} '''Ubanato Shrine''' (е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ) is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|е…ғдјҠеӢў|lt=Moto-Ise shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} located in Anpachi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}, representing Ubanato Shrine in Anpachi District, Mino Province. Another candidate is {{ill|Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (зҘһжҲёз”ә)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|qq=}}. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the Rank of a GЕҚsha. {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|е…ғдјҠеӢў|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} are [[Shinto shrine]]s that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]].{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=7pEOicqsjv0C&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA201|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai: Archaeology, History, and Mythology|date=2007-02-28|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3035-9|language=en}}{{Cite book|last=Stalker|first=Nancy K.|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=v44BEAAAQBAJ&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA62|title=Prophet Motive: Deguchi OnisaburЕҚ, Oomoto, and the Rise of New Religions in Imperial Japan|date=2007-10-31|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6404-0|language=en}} The first one was [[:en:ЕҢmiwa Shrine|Hibara Shrine]].{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2011-08-10|title=Hibara Jinja and Amaterasu|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2011/08/11/hibara-jinja-and-amaterasu/|access-date=2023-10-18|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}{{Cite web|title=Hibara Jinja ShrineпјҲNaraпјү|url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/en/location/290100006/?search=1&q=Hibara+Shrine|access-date=2023-10-03|website=Nationwide location database|language=en}} ==History== The founding date is unknown. According to tradition, when Princess Yamatohime-no-mikoto was searching for a suitable place to enshrine Amaterasu during the reign of Emperor Suinin, she passed through this site while traveling from {{ill|Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|ja|еӨ©зҘһзҘһзӨҫ (з‘һз©ӮеёӮ)|lt=Ikurakawa-no-miya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|qq=}} in Mino Province to Owari Province. Another theory suggests that this shrine itself was originally Ikurakawa-no-miya, supported by an inscription found on the reverse side of its treasured sacred mirror. The area historically suffered frequent flood damage. The original location of the shrine is unclear, but records indicate relocation occurred in 1603. During the Edo period, the shrine was under the protection of Owari Domain. *In 1924, the shrine relocated due to upstream construction work on the Kiso River. *In 1933, relocated again due to embankment work on the Nagara River. *In 1934, relocated to its current site due to reconstruction work on the Sai River. ==Enshrined Deities== *[[Amaterasu]] *[[Toyouke-hime]] *[[Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] *Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami ==Transportation== *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Gikaki Line toward Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University or Gifu Distribution Center). *Approx. 2.5 km from "Minami-Omori" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Hashima Line toward Gifu-Hashima Station). *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Gifu Bus Obusa-Sunomata Line). *5-minute walk from "Moribe" bus stop (Anpachi Community Bus). ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)|version=77963473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Anpachi, Gifu]] [[Category:History of Anpachi, Gifu]] }} [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]] [[en:draft:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]] [[ja:е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)]] [[Category:е®үе…«з”әгҒ®е»әзҜүзү©]]

Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on EverybodyWiki here [[:every:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)|Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a Wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to EverybodyWiki and recovered from there. In the future, it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the EverybodyWiki page may have been under a different title. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Content originally from [[Draft:Moto-Ise Shrines]]. See that page history for attribution. [[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuelle''']] вқӨпёҸрҹ’ҡрҹ’ҷ [[User talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 06:22, 3 December 2023 (UTC)}} {{infobox shrine | Name = Ubanato Shrine | image = [[File:Uwato anpachi.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine building | Location = 373-10 Moribe, Anpachi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture | Coordinates = {{coord|35|20|37.35|N|136|41|5.70|E|scale:20000}} | Deities = [[Amaterasu]], [[Toyouke-hime]], [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami | Shrine Rank = Shikinaisha (Small), GЕҚsha | Established = Unknown }} '''Ubanato Shrine''' (е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ) is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|е…ғдјҠеӢў|lt=Moto-Ise shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} located in Anpachi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}, representing Ubanato Shrine in Anpachi District, Mino Province. Another candidate is {{ill|Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (зҘһжҲёз”ә)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|qq=}}. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the Rank of a GЕҚsha. {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|е…ғдјҠеӢў|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} are [[Shinto shrine]]s that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]].{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=7pEOicqsjv0C&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA201|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai: Archaeology, History, and Mythology|date=2007-02-28|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3035-9|language=en}}{{Cite book|last=Stalker|first=Nancy K.|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=v44BEAAAQBAJ&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA62|title=Prophet Motive: Deguchi OnisaburЕҚ, Oomoto, and the Rise of New Religions in Imperial Japan|date=2007-10-31|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6404-0|language=en}} The first one was [[:en:ЕҢmiwa Shrine|Hibara Shrine]].{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2011-08-10|title=Hibara Jinja and Amaterasu|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2011/08/11/hibara-jinja-and-amaterasu/|access-date=2023-10-18|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}{{Cite web|title=Hibara Jinja ShrineпјҲNaraпјү|url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/en/location/290100006/?search=1&q=Hibara+Shrine|access-date=2023-10-03|website=Nationwide location database|language=en}} ==History== The founding date is unknown. According to tradition, when Princess Yamatohime-no-mikoto was searching for a suitable place to enshrine Amaterasu during the reign of Emperor Suinin, she passed through this site while traveling from {{ill|Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|ja|еӨ©зҘһзҘһзӨҫ (з‘һз©ӮеёӮ)|lt=Ikurakawa-no-miya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|qq=}} in Mino Province to Owari Province. Another theory suggests that this shrine itself was originally Ikurakawa-no-miya, supported by an inscription found on the reverse side of its treasured sacred mirror. The area historically suffered frequent flood damage. The original location of the shrine is unclear, but records indicate relocation occurred in 1603. During the Edo period, the shrine was under the protection of Owari Domain. *In 1924, the shrine relocated due to upstream construction work on the Kiso River. *In 1933, relocated again due to embankment work on the Nagara River. *In 1934, relocated to its current site due to reconstruction work on the Sai River. ==Enshrined Deities== *[[Amaterasu]] *[[Toyouke-hime]] *[[Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] *Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami ==Transportation== *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Gikaki Line toward Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University or Gifu Distribution Center). *Approx. 2.5 km from "Minami-Omori" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Hashima Line toward Gifu-Hashima Station). *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Gifu Bus Obusa-Sunomata Line). *5-minute walk from "Moribe" bus stop (Anpachi Community Bus). ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)|version=77963473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Anpachi, Gifu]] [[Category:History of Anpachi, Gifu]] }} [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]] [[ja:е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)]] [[Category:е®үе…«з”әгҒ®е»әзҜүзү©]] [[Category:е®үе…«з”әгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]]

Another candidate is {{ill|Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (зҘһжҲёз”ә)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|qq=}}. During the Edo period, the shrine was under the protection of Owari Domain. *In 1924, the shrine relocated due to upstream construction work on the Kiso River. *In 1933, relocated again due to embankment work on the Nagara River. *In 1934, relocated to its current site due to reconstruction work on the Sai River. ==Enshrined Deities== *[[Amaterasu]] *[[Toyouke-hime]] *[[Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] *Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami ==Transportation== *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Gikaki Line toward Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University or Gifu Distribution Center). *Approx. 2.5 km from "Minami-Omori" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Hashima Line toward Gifu-Hashima Station). *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Gifu Bus Obusa-Sunomata Line). *5-minute walk from "Moribe" bus stop (Anpachi Community Bus). ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)|version=77963473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Anpachi, Gifu]] }} [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]] [[ja:е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)]] [[Category:е®үе…«з”әгҒ®е»әзҜүзү©]] [[Category:е®үе…«з”әгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]]

{{afc comment|1=Page originally made on English Wikipedia in draftspace. G13 deleted and archived on EverybodyWiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a Wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to EverybodyWiki and recovered from there. In the future, it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the EverybodyWiki page may have been under a different title. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Content originally from [[Draft:Moto-Ise Shrines]]. See that page history for attribution. [[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuelle''']] вқӨпёҸрҹ’ҡрҹ’ҷ [[User talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 06:22, 3 December 2023 (UTC)}} {{infobox shrine | Name = Ubanato Shrine | image = [[File:Uwato anpachi.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine building | Location = 373-10 Moribe, Anpachi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture | Coordinates = {{coord|35|20|37.35|N|136|41|5.70|E|scale:20000}} | Deities = [[Amaterasu]], [[Toyouke-hime]], [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami | Shrine Rank = Shikinaisha (Small), GЕҚsha | Established = Unknown }} '''Ubanato Shrine''' (е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ) is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|е…ғдјҠеӢў|lt=Moto-Ise shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} located in Anpachi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}, representing Ubanato Shrine in Anpachi District, Mino Province. Another candidate is {{ill|Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (зҘһжҲёз”ә)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|qq=}}. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the Rank of a GЕҚsha. {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|е…ғдјҠеӢў|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} are [[Shinto shrine]]s that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]].{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=7pEOicqsjv0C&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA201|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai: Archaeology, History, and Mythology|date=2007-02-28|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3035-9|language=en}}{{Cite book|last=Stalker|first=Nancy K.|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=v44BEAAAQBAJ&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA62|title=Prophet Motive: Deguchi OnisaburЕҚ, Oomoto, and the Rise of New Religions in Imperial Japan|date=2007-10-31|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6404-0|language=en}} The first one was [[:en:ЕҢmiwa Shrine|Hibara Shrine]].{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2011-08-10|title=Hibara Jinja and Amaterasu|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2011/08/11/hibara-jinja-and-amaterasu/|access-date=2023-10-18|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}{{Cite web|title=Hibara Jinja ShrineпјҲNaraпјү|url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/en/location/290100006/?search=1&q=Hibara+Shrine|access-date=2023-10-03|website=Nationwide location database|language=en}} ==History== The founding date is unknown. According to tradition, when Princess Yamatohime-no-mikoto was searching for a suitable place to enshrine Amaterasu during the reign of Emperor Suinin, she passed through this site while traveling from {{ill|Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|ja|еӨ©зҘһзҘһзӨҫ (з‘һз©ӮеёӮ)|lt=Ikurakawa-no-miya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|qq=}} in Mino Province to Owari Province. Another theory suggests that this shrine itself was originally Ikurakawa-no-miya, supported by an inscription found on the reverse side of its treasured sacred mirror. The area historically suffered frequent flood damage. The original location of the shrine is unclear, but records indicate relocation occurred in 1603. During the Edo period, the shrine was under the protection of Owari Domain. *In 1924, the shrine relocated due to upstream construction work on the Kiso River. *In 1933, relocated again due to embankment work on the Nagara River. *In 1934, relocated to its current site due to reconstruction work on the Sai River. ==Enshrined Deities== *[[Amaterasu]] *[[Toyouke-hime]] *[[Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] *Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami ==Transportation== *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Gikaki Line toward Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University or Gifu Distribution Center). *Approx. 2.5 km from "Minami-Omori" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Hashima Line toward Gifu-Hashima Station). *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Gifu Bus Obusa-Sunomata Line). *5-minute walk from "Moribe" bus stop (Anpachi Community Bus). ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)|version=77963473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]] [[ja:е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)]] [[Category:е®үе…«з”әгҒ®е»әзҜүзү©]] [[Category:е®үе…«з”әгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]]

Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the Rank of a GЕҚsha. {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|е…ғдјҠеӢў|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} are [[Shinto shrine]]s that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]].{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=7pEOicqsjv0C&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA201|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai: Archaeology, History, and Mythology|date=2007-02-28|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3035-9|language=en}}{{Cite book|last=Stalker|first=Nancy K.|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=v44BEAAAQBAJ&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA62|title=Prophet Motive: Deguchi OnisaburЕҚ, Oomoto, and the Rise of New Religions in Imperial Japan|date=2007-10-31|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6404-0|language=en}} The first one was [[:en:ЕҢmiwa Shrine|Hibara Shrine]].{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2011-08-10|title=Hibara Jinja and Amaterasu|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2011/08/11/hibara-jinja-and-amaterasu/|access-date=2023-10-18|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}{{Cite web|title=Hibara Jinja ShrineпјҲNaraпјү|url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/en/location/290100006/?search=1&q=Hibara+Shrine|access-date=2023-10-03|website=Nationwide location database|language=en}} ==History== The founding date is unknown. According to tradition, when Princess Yamatohime-no-mikoto was searching for a suitable place to enshrine Amaterasu during the reign of Emperor Suinin, she passed through this site while traveling from {{ill|Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|ja|еӨ©зҘһзҘһзӨҫ (з‘һз©ӮеёӮ)|lt=Ikurakawa-no-miya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|qq=}} in Mino Province to Owari Province. Another theory suggests that this shrine itself was originally Ikurakawa-no-miya, supported by an inscription found on the reverse side of its treasured sacred mirror. The area historically suffered frequent flood damage. The original location of the shrine is unclear, but records indicate relocation occurred in 1603. During the Edo period, the shrine was under the protection of Owari Domain. *In 1924, the shrine relocated due to upstream construction work on the Kiso River. *In 1933, relocated again due to embankment work on the Nagara River. *In 1934, relocated to its current site due to reconstruction work on the Sai River. ==Enshrined Deities== *[[Amaterasu]] *[[Toyouke-hime]] *[[Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] *Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami ==Transportation== *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Gikaki Line toward Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University or Gifu Distribution Center). *Approx. 2.5 km from "Minami-Omori" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Hashima Line toward Gifu-Hashima Station). *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Gifu Bus Obusa-Sunomata Line). *5-minute walk from "Moribe" bus stop (Anpachi Community Bus). ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)|version=77963473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]] [[ja:е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)]] [[Category:е®үе…«з”әгҒ®е»әзҜүзү©]] [[Category:Municipal History of Anpachi]]

It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}, representing Ubanato Shrine in Anpachi District, Mino Province. Another candidate is {{ill|Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (зҘһжҲёз”ә)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|qq=}}. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the Rank of a GЕҚsha. {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|е…ғдјҠеӢў|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} are [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]].{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=7pEOicqsjv0C&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA201|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai: Archaeology, History, and Mythology|date=2007-02-28|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3035-9|language=en}}{{Cite book|last=Stalker|first=Nancy K.|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=v44BEAAAQBAJ&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA62|title=Prophet Motive: Deguchi OnisaburЕҚ, Oomoto, and the Rise of New Religions in Imperial Japan|date=2007-10-31|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6404-0|language=en}} The first one was [[:en:ЕҢmiwa Shrine|Hibara Shrine]].{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2011-08-10|title=Hibara Jinja and Amaterasu|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2011/08/11/hibara-jinja-and-amaterasu/|access-date=2023-10-18|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}{{Cite web|title=Hibara Jinja ShrineпјҲNaraпјү|url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/en/location/290100006/?search=1&q=Hibara+Shrine|access-date=2023-10-03|website=Nationwide location database|language=en}} ==History== The founding date is unknown. According to tradition, when Princess Yamatohime-no-mikoto was searching for a suitable place to enshrine Amaterasu during the reign of Emperor Suinin, she passed through this site while traveling from {{ill|Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|ja|еӨ©зҘһзҘһзӨҫ (з‘һз©ӮеёӮ)|lt=Ikurakawa-no-miya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|qq=}} in Mino Province to Owari Province. Another theory suggests that this shrine itself was originally Ikurakawa-no-miya, supported by an inscription found on the reverse side of its treasured sacred mirror. The area historically suffered frequent flood damage. The original location of the shrine is unclear, but records indicate relocation occurred in 1603. During the Edo period, the shrine was under the protection of Owari Domain. *In 1924, the shrine relocated due to upstream construction work on the Kiso River. *In 1933, relocated again due to embankment work on the Nagara River. *In 1934, relocated to its current site due to reconstruction work on the Sai River. ==Enshrined Deities== *[[Amaterasu]] *[[Toyouke-hime]] *[[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] *Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami ==Transportation== *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Gikaki Line toward Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University or Gifu Distribution Center). *Approx. 2.5 km from "Minami-Omori" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Hashima Line toward Gifu-Hashima Station). *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Gifu Bus Obusa-Sunomata Line). *5-minute walk from "Moribe" bus stop (Anpachi Community Bus). ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)|version=77963473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]] [[ja:е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)]] [[Category:е®үе…«з”әгҒ®е»әзҜүзү©]] [[Category:Municipal History of Anpachi]]

Main shrine building | Location = 373-10 Moribe, Anpachi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture | Coordinates = {{coord|35|20|37.35|N|136|41|5.70|E|scale:20000}} | Deities = [[Amaterasu]], [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]], [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami | Shrine Rank = Shikinaisha (Small), GЕҚsha | Established = Unknown }} '''Ubanato Shrine''' (е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ) is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|е…ғдјҠеӢў|lt=Moto-Ise shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} located in Anpachi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}, representing Ubanato Shrine in Anpachi District, Mino Province. Another candidate is {{ill|Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (зҘһжҲёз”ә)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|qq=}}. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the Rank of a GЕҚsha. {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|е…ғдјҠеӢў|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} are [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]].{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=7pEOicqsjv0C&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA201|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai: Archaeology, History, and Mythology|date=2007-02-28|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3035-9|language=en}}{{Cite book|last=Stalker|first=Nancy K.|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=v44BEAAAQBAJ&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA62|title=Prophet Motive: Deguchi OnisaburЕҚ, Oomoto, and the Rise of New Religions in Imperial Japan|date=2007-10-31|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6404-0|language=en}} The first one was [[:en:ЕҢmiwa Shrine|Hibara Shrine]].{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2011-08-10|title=Hibara Jinja and Amaterasu|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2011/08/11/hibara-jinja-and-amaterasu/|access-date=2023-10-18|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}{{Cite web|title=Hibara Jinja ShrineпјҲNaraпјү|url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/en/location/290100006/?search=1&q=Hibara+Shrine|access-date=2023-10-03|website=Nationwide location database|language=en}} ==History== The founding date is unknown. According to tradition, when Princess Yamatohime-no-mikoto was searching for a suitable place to enshrine Amaterasu during the reign of Emperor Suinin, she passed through this site while traveling from {{ill|Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|ja|еӨ©зҘһзҘһзӨҫ (з‘һз©ӮеёӮ)|lt=Ikurakawa-no-miya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|qq=}} in Mino Province to Owari Province. Another theory suggests that this shrine itself was originally Ikurakawa-no-miya, supported by an inscription found on the reverse side of its treasured sacred mirror. The area historically suffered frequent flood damage. The original location of the shrine is unclear, but records indicate relocation occurred in 1603. During the Edo period, the shrine was under the protection of Owari Domain. *In 1924, the shrine relocated due to upstream construction work on the Kiso River. *In 1933, relocated again due to embankment work on the Nagara River. *In 1934, relocated to its current site due to reconstruction work on the Sai River. ==Enshrined Deities== *[[Amaterasu]] *[[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] *[[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] *Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami ==Transportation== *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Gikaki Line toward Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University or Gifu Distribution Center). *Approx. 2.5 km from "Minami-Omori" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Hashima Line toward Gifu-Hashima Station). *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Gifu Bus Obusa-Sunomata Line). *5-minute walk from "Moribe" bus stop (Anpachi Community Bus). ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)|version=77963473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]] [[ja:е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)]] [[Category:Buildings in Anpachi Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Anpachi]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Edward|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=7pEOicqsjv0C&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA201|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai: Archaeology, History, and Mythology|date=2007-02-28|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3035-9|language=en}}{{Cite book|last=Stalker|first=Nancy K.|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=v44BEAAAQBAJ&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA62|title=Prophet Motive: Deguchi OnisaburЕҚ, Oomoto, and the Rise of New Religions in Imperial Japan|date=2007-10-31|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6404-0|language=en}} The first one was [[:en:ЕҢmiwa Shrine|Hibara Shrine]].{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2011-08-10|title=Hibara Jinja and Amaterasu|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2011/08/11/hibara-jinja-and-amaterasu/|access-date=2023-10-18|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}{{Cite web|title=Hibara Jinja ShrineпјҲNaraпјү|url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/en/location/290100006/?search=1&q=Hibara+Shrine|access-date=2023-10-03|website=Nationwide location database|language=en}} ==History== The founding date is unknown. According to tradition, when Princess Yamatohime-no-mikoto was searching for a suitable place to enshrine Amaterasu during the reign of Emperor Suinin, she passed through this site while traveling from {{ill|Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|ja|еӨ©зҘһзҘһзӨҫ (з‘һз©ӮеёӮ)|lt=Ikurakawa-no-miya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|qq=}} in Mino Province to Owari Province. Another theory suggests that this shrine itself was originally Ikurakawa-no-miya, supported by an inscription found on the reverse side of its treasured sacred mirror. The area historically suffered frequent flood damage. The original location of the shrine is unclear, but records indicate relocation occurred in 1603. During the Edo period, the shrine was under the protection of Owari Domain. *In 1924, the shrine relocated due to upstream construction work on the Kiso River. *In 1933, relocated again due to embankment work on the Nagara River. *In 1934, relocated to its current site due to reconstruction work on the Sai River. ==Enshrined Deities== *[[Amaterasu]] *[[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] *[[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] *Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami ==Transportation== *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Gikaki Line toward Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University or Gifu Distribution Center). *Approx. 2.5 km from "Minami-Omori" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Hashima Line toward Gifu-Hashima Station). *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Gifu Bus Obusa-Sunomata Line). *5-minute walk from "Moribe" bus stop (Anpachi Community Bus). ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)|version=77963473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]] [[ja:е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)]] [[Category:Buildings in Anpachi Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Anpachi]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Main shrine building | Location = 373-10 Moribe, Anpachi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture | Coordinates = {{coord|35|20|37.35|N|136|41|5.70|E|scale:20000}} | Deities = [[Amaterasu]], [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]], [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami | Shrine Rank = Shikinaisha (Small), GЕҚsha | Established = Unknown }} '''Ubanato Shrine''' (е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ) is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|е…ғдјҠеӢў|lt=Moto-Ise shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} located in Anpachi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}, representing Ubanato Shrine in Anpachi District, Mino Province. Another candidate is {{ill|Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (зҘһжҲёз”ә)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|qq=|1=Uwato Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217996}}. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the Rank of a GЕҚsha. {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|е…ғдјҠеӢў|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} are [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]].{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=7pEOicqsjv0C&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA201|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai: Archaeology, History, and Mythology|date=2007-02-28|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3035-9|language=en}}{{Cite book|last=Stalker|first=Nancy K.|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=v44BEAAAQBAJ&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA62|title=Prophet Motive: Deguchi OnisaburЕҚ, Oomoto, and the Rise of New Religions in Imperial Japan|date=2007-10-31|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6404-0|language=en}} The first one was [[:en:ЕҢmiwa Shrine|Hibara Shrine]].{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2011-08-10|title=Hibara Jinja and Amaterasu|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2011/08/11/hibara-jinja-and-amaterasu/|access-date=2023-10-18|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}{{Cite web|title=Hibara Jinja ShrineпјҲNaraпјү|url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/en/location/290100006/?search=1&q=Hibara+Shrine|access-date=2023-10-03|website=Nationwide location database|language=en}} ==History== The founding date is unknown. According to tradition, when Princess Yamatohime-no-mikoto was searching for a suitable place to enshrine Amaterasu during the reign of Emperor Suinin, she passed through this site while traveling from {{ill|Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|ja|еӨ©зҘһзҘһзӨҫ (з‘һз©ӮеёӮ)|lt=Ikurakawa-no-miya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|qq=|1=Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443289}} in Mino Province to Owari Province. Another theory suggests that this shrine itself was originally Ikurakawa-no-miya, supported by an inscription found on the reverse side of its treasured sacred mirror. The area historically suffered frequent flood damage. The original location of the shrine is unclear, but records indicate relocation occurred in 1603. During the Edo period, the shrine was under the protection of Owari Domain. *In 1924, the shrine relocated due to upstream construction work on the Kiso River. *In 1933, relocated again due to embankment work on the Nagara River. *In 1934, relocated to its current site due to reconstruction work on the Sai River. ==Enshrined Deities== *[[Amaterasu]] *[[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] *[[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] *Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami ==Transportation== *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Gikaki Line toward Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University or Gifu Distribution Center). *Approx. 2.5 km from "Minami-Omori" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Hashima Line toward Gifu-Hashima Station). *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Gifu Bus Obusa-Sunomata Line). *5-minute walk from "Moribe" bus stop (Anpachi Community Bus). ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)|version=77963473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]] [[ja:е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)]] [[Category:Buildings in Anpachi Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Anpachi]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]][[Category:qq]]

Another theory suggests that this shrine itself was originally Ikurakawa-no-miya, supported by an inscription found on the reverse side of its treasured sacred mirror. The area historically suffered frequent flood damage. The original location of the shrine is unclear, but records indicate relocation occurred in 1603. During the Edo period, the shrine was under the protection of Owari Domain. *In 1924, the shrine relocated due to upstream construction work on the Kiso River. *In 1933, relocated again due to embankment work on the Nagara River. *In 1934, relocated to its current site due to reconstruction work on the Sai River. ==Enshrined Deities== *[[Amaterasu]] *[[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] *[[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] *Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami ==Transportation== *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Gikaki Line toward Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University or Gifu Distribution Center). *Approx. 2.5 km from "Minami-Omori" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Hashima Line toward Gifu-Hashima Station). *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Gifu Bus Obusa-Sunomata Line). *5-minute walk from "Moribe" bus stop (Anpachi Community Bus). ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)|version=77963473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]] [[ja:е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)]] [[Category:Buildings in Anpachi Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Anpachi]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]][[Category:qq]]

It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}, representing Ubanato Shrine in Anpachi District, Mino Province. Another candidate is {{ill|Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (зҘһжҲёз”ә)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|qq=|1=Uwato Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217996}}. It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}, representing Ubanato Shrine in Anpachi District, Mino Province. Another candidate is {{ill|Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (зҘһжҲёз”ә)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|qq=|1=Uwato Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217996}}. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the Rank of a GЕҚsha. {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|е…ғдјҠеӢў|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} are [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]].{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=7pEOicqsjv0C&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA201|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai: Archaeology, History, and Mythology|date=2007-02-28|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3035-9|language=en}}{{Cite book|last=Stalker|first=Nancy K.|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=v44BEAAAQBAJ&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA62|title=Prophet Motive: Deguchi OnisaburЕҚ, Oomoto, and the Rise of New Religions in Imperial Japan|date=2007-10-31|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6404-0|language=en}} The first one was [[:en:ЕҢmiwa Shrine|Hibara Shrine]].{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2011-08-10|title=Hibara Jinja and Amaterasu|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2011/08/11/hibara-jinja-and-amaterasu/|access-date=2023-10-18|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}{{Cite web|title=Hibara Jinja ShrineпјҲNaraпјү|url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/en/location/290100006/?search=1&q=Hibara+Shrine|access-date=2023-10-03|website=Nationwide location database|language=en}} ==History== The founding date is unknown. According to tradition, when Princess Yamatohime-no-mikoto was searching for a suitable place to enshrine Amaterasu during the reign of Emperor Suinin, she passed through this site while traveling from {{ill|Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|ja|еӨ©зҘһзҘһзӨҫ (з‘һз©ӮеёӮ)|lt=Ikurakawa-no-miya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|qq=|1=Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443289}} in Mino Province to Owari Province. Another theory suggests that this shrine itself was originally Ikurakawa-no-miya, supported by an inscription found on the reverse side of its treasured sacred mirror. The area historically suffered frequent flood damage. The original location of the shrine is unclear, but records indicate relocation occurred in 1603. During the Edo period, the shrine was under the protection of Owari Domain. *In 1924, the shrine relocated due to upstream construction work on the Kiso River. *In 1933, relocated again due to embankment work on the Nagara River. *In 1934, relocated to its current site due to reconstruction work on the Sai River. ==Enshrined Deities== *[[Amaterasu]] *[[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] *[[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] *Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami ==Transportation== *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Gikaki Line toward Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University or Gifu Distribution Center). *Approx. 2.5 km from "Minami-Omori" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Hashima Line toward Gifu-Hashima Station). *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Gifu Bus Obusa-Sunomata Line). *5-minute walk from "Moribe" bus stop (Anpachi Community Bus). ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)|version=77963473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]] [[ja:е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)]] [[Category:Buildings in Anpachi Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Anpachi]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

The area historically suffered frequent flood damage. The original location of the shrine is unclear, but records indicate relocation occurred in 1603. During the Edo period, the shrine was under the protection of Owari Domain. *In 1924, the shrine relocated due to upstream construction work on the Kiso River. *In 1933, relocated again due to embankment work on the Nagara River. *In 1934, relocated to its current site due to reconstruction work on the Sai River. ==Enshrined Deities== *[[Amaterasu]] *[[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] *[[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] *Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami ==Transportation== *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Gikaki Line toward Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University or Gifu Distribution Center). *Approx. 2.5 km from "Minami-Omori" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Hashima Line toward Gifu-Hashima Station). *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Gifu Bus Obusa-Sunomata Line). *5-minute walk from "Moribe" bus stop (Anpachi Community Bus). ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)|version=77963473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]] [[ja:е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)]] [[Category:Buildings in Anpachi Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Anpachi]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}, representing Ubanato Shrine in Anpachi District, Mino Province. Another candidate is {{ill|Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (зҘһжҲёз”ә)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|qq=|1=Uwato Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217996}}. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the Rank of a GЕҚsha. {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|е…ғдјҠеӢў|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} are [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]].{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. The area historically suffered frequent flood damage. The original location of the shrine is unclear, but records indicate relocation occurred in 1603. During the Edo period, the shrine was under the protection of Owari Domain. *In 1924, the shrine relocated due to upstream construction work on the Kiso River. *In 1933, relocated again due to embankment work on the Nagara River. *In 1934, relocated to its current site due to reconstruction work on the Sai River. ==Enshrined Deities== *[[Amaterasu]] *[[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] *[[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] *Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami ==Transportation== *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Gikaki Line toward Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University or Gifu Distribution Center). *Approx. 2.5 km from "Minami-Omori" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Hashima Line toward Gifu-Hashima Station). *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Gifu Bus Obusa-Sunomata Line). *5-minute walk from "Moribe" bus stop (Anpachi Community Bus). ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)|version=77963473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]] [[ja:е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)]] [[Category:Buildings in Anpachi Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Anpachi]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}, representing Ubanato Shrine in Anpachi District, Mino Province. Another candidate is {{ill|Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (зҘһжҲёз”ә)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|qq=|1=Uwato Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217996}}. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the Rank of a GЕҚsha. {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|е…ғдјҠеӢў|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} are [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]].{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=7pEOicqsjv0C&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA201|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai: Archaeology, History, and Mythology|date=2007-02-28|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3035-9|language=en}}{{Cite book|last=Stalker|first=Nancy K.|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=v44BEAAAQBAJ&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA62|title=Prophet Motive: Deguchi OnisaburЕҚ, Oomoto, and the Rise of New Religions in Imperial Japan|date=2007-10-31|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6404-0|language=en}} The first one was [[:en:ЕҢmiwa Shrine|Hibara Shrine]].{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2011-08-10|title=Hibara Jinja and Amaterasu|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2011/08/11/hibara-jinja-and-amaterasu/|access-date=2023-10-18|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}{{Cite web|title=Hibara Jinja ShrineпјҲNaraпјү|url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/en/location/290100006/?search=1&q=Hibara+Shrine|access-date=2023-10-03|website=Nationwide location database|language=en}} ==History== The founding date is unknown. According to tradition, when Princess Yamatohime-no-mikoto was searching for a suitable place to enshrine Amaterasu during the reign of Emperor Suinin, she passed through this site while traveling from {{ill|Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|ja|еӨ©зҘһзҘһзӨҫ (з‘һз©ӮеёӮ)|lt=Ikurakawa-no-miya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|qq=|1=Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443289}} in Mino Province to Owari Province. Another theory suggests that this shrine itself was originally Ikurakawa-no-miya, supported by an inscription found on the reverse side of its treasured sacred mirror. The area historically suffered frequent flood damage. The original location of the shrine is unclear, but records indicate relocation occurred in 1603. During the Edo period, the shrine was under the protection of Owari Domain. *In 1924, the shrine relocated due to upstream construction work on the Kiso River. *In 1933, relocated again due to embankment work on the Nagara River. *In 1934, relocated to its current site due to reconstruction work on the Sai River. ==Enshrined Deities== *[[Amaterasu]] *[[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] *[[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] *Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami ==Transportation== *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Gikaki Line toward Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University or Gifu Distribution Center). *Approx. 2.5 km from "Minami-Omori" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Hashima Line toward Gifu-Hashima Station). *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Gifu Bus Obusa-Sunomata Line). *5-minute walk from "Moribe" bus stop (Anpachi Community Bus). ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)|version=77963473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]] [[ja:е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)]] [[Category:Buildings in Anpachi Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Anpachi]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

According to tradition, when Princess Yamatohime-no-mikoto was searching for a suitable place to enshrine Amaterasu during the reign of Emperor Suinin, she passed through this site while traveling from {{ill|Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|ja|еӨ©зҘһзҘһзӨҫ (з‘һз©ӮеёӮ)|lt=Ikurakawa-no-miya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|qq=|1=Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443289}} in Mino Province to Owari Province. Another theory suggests that this shrine itself was originally Ikurakawa-no-miya, supported by an inscription found on the reverse side of its treasured sacred mirror. The area historically suffered frequent flood damage. The original location of the shrine is unclear, but records indicate relocation occurred in 1603. During the Edo period, the shrine was under the protection of Owari Domain. *In 1924, the shrine relocated due to upstream construction work on the Kiso River. *In 1933, relocated again due to embankment work on the Nagara River. *In 1934, relocated to its current site due to reconstruction work on the Sai River. ==Enshrined Deities== *[[Amaterasu]] *[[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] *[[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] *Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami ==Transportation== *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Gikaki Line toward Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University or Gifu Distribution Center). *Approx. 2.5 km from "Minami-Omori" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Hashima Line toward Gifu-Hashima Station). *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Gifu Bus Obusa-Sunomata Line). *5-minute walk from "Moribe" bus stop (Anpachi Community Bus). ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)|version=77963473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]] [[ja:е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)]] [[Category:Buildings in Anpachi Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Anpachi]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)]]

It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}, representing Ubanato Shrine in Anpachi District, Mino Province. Another candidate is {{ill|Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (зҘһжҲёз”ә)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|qq=|1=Uwato Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217996}}. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the Rank of a GЕҚsha. {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|е…ғдјҠеӢў|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} are [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]].{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=7pEOicqsjv0C&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA201|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai: Archaeology, History, and Mythology|date=2007-02-28|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3035-9|language=en}}{{Cite book|last=Stalker|first=Nancy K.|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=v44BEAAAQBAJ&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA62|title=Prophet Motive: Deguchi OnisaburЕҚ, Oomoto, and the Rise of New Religions in Imperial Japan|date=2007-10-31|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6404-0|language=en}} The first one was [[:en:ЕҢmiwa Shrine|Hibara Shrine]].{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2011-08-10|title=Hibara Jinja and Amaterasu|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2011/08/11/hibara-jinja-and-amaterasu/|access-date=2023-10-18|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}{{Cite web|title=Hibara Jinja ShrineпјҲNaraпјү|url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/en/location/290100006/?search=1&q=Hibara+Shrine|access-date=2023-10-03|website=Nationwide location database|language=en}} ==History== The founding date is unknown. According to tradition, when Princess Yamatohime-no-mikoto was searching for a suitable place to enshrine Amaterasu during the reign of Emperor Suinin, she passed through this site while traveling from {{ill|Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|ja|еӨ©зҘһзҘһзӨҫ (з‘һз©ӮеёӮ)|lt=Ikurakawa-no-miya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|qq=|1=Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443289}} in Mino Province to Owari Province. Another theory suggests that this shrine itself was originally Ikurakawa-no-miya, supported by an inscription found on the reverse side of its treasured sacred mirror. The area historically suffered frequent flood damage. The original location of the shrine is unclear, but records indicate relocation occurred in 1603. During the Edo period, the shrine was under the protection of Owari Domain. *In 1924, the shrine relocated due to upstream construction work on the Kiso River. *In 1933, relocated again due to embankment work on the Nagara River. *In 1934, relocated to its current site due to reconstruction work on the Sai River. ==Enshrined Deities== *[[Amaterasu]] *[[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] *[[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] *Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami ==Transportation== *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Gikaki Line toward Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University or Gifu Distribution Center). *Approx. 2.5 km from "Minami-Omori" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Hashima Line toward Gifu-Hashima Station). *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Gifu Bus Obusa-Sunomata Line). *5-minute walk from "Moribe" bus stop (Anpachi Community Bus). ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)|version=77963473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] }} [[ja:е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)]] [[Category:Buildings in Anpachi Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Anpachi]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11449527}}

During the Edo period, the shrine was under the protection of Owari Domain. *In 1924, the shrine relocated due to upstream construction work on the Kiso River. *In 1933, relocated again due to embankment work on the Nagara River. *In 1934, relocated to its current site due to reconstruction work on the Sai River. ==Enshrined Deities== *[[Amaterasu]] *[[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] *[[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] *Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami ==Transportation== *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Gikaki Line toward Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University or Gifu Distribution Center). *Approx. 2.5 km from "Minami-Omori" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Hashima Line toward Gifu-Hashima Station). *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Gifu Bus Obusa-Sunomata Line). *5-minute walk from "Moribe" bus stop (Anpachi Community Bus). ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)|version=77963473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] }} [[ja:е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)]] [[Category:Buildings in Anpachi Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Anpachi]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11449527}}

Main shrine building | Location = 373-10 Moribe, Anpachi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture | Coordinates = {{coord|35|20|37.35|N|136|41|5.70|E|scale:20000}} | Deities = [[Amaterasu]], [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]], [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami | Shrine Rank = Shikinaisha (Small), GЕҚsha | Established = Unknown }} '''Ubanato Shrine''' (е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ) is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|е…ғдјҠеӢў|lt=Moto-Ise shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} located in Anpachi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}, representing Ubanato Shrine in Anpachi District, Mino Province. Another candidate is {{ill|Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (зҘһжҲёз”ә)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|qq=|1=Uwato Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217996}}. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the Rank of a GЕҚsha. {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|е…ғдјҠеӢў|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} are [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]].{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=7pEOicqsjv0C&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA201|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai: Archaeology, History, and Mythology|date=2007-02-28|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3035-9|language=en}}{{Cite book|last=Stalker|first=Nancy K.|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=v44BEAAAQBAJ&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA62|title=Prophet Motive: Deguchi OnisaburЕҚ, Oomoto, and the Rise of New Religions in Imperial Japan|date=2007-10-31|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6404-0|language=en}} The first one was [[:en:ЕҢmiwa Shrine|Hibara Shrine]].{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2011-08-10|title=Hibara Jinja and Amaterasu|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2011/08/11/hibara-jinja-and-amaterasu/|access-date=2023-10-18|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}{{Cite web|title=Hibara Jinja ShrineпјҲNaraпјү|url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/en/location/290100006/?search=1&q=Hibara+Shrine|access-date=2023-10-03|website=Nationwide location database|language=en}} ==History== The founding date is unknown. According to tradition, when Princess Yamatohime-no-mikoto was searching for a suitable place to enshrine Amaterasu during the reign of Emperor Suinin, she passed through this site while traveling from {{ill|Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|ja|еӨ©зҘһзҘһзӨҫ (з‘һз©ӮеёӮ)|lt=Ikurakawa-no-miya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|qq=|1=Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443289}} in Mino Province to Owari Province. Another theory suggests that this shrine itself was originally Ikurakawa-no-miya, supported by an inscription found on the reverse side of its treasured sacred mirror. The area historically suffered frequent flood damage. The original location of the shrine is unclear, but records indicate relocation occurred in 1603. During the Edo period, the shrine was under the protection of Owari Domain. *In 1924, the shrine relocated due to upstream construction work on the Kiso River. *In 1933, relocated again due to embankment work on the Nagara River. *In 1934, relocated to its current site due to reconstruction work on the Sai River. ==Enshrined Deities== *[[Amaterasu]] *[[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] *[[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] *Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami ==Transportation== *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Gikaki Line toward Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University or Gifu Distribution Center). *Approx. 2.5 km from "Minami-Omori" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Hashima Line toward Gifu-Hashima Station). *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Gifu Bus Obusa-Sunomata Line). *5-minute walk from "Moribe" bus stop (Anpachi Community Bus). ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)|version=77963473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] }} [[ja:е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)]] [[Category:Buildings in Anpachi Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Anpachi]] {{wikidata link|Q11449527}}
