Main shrine building | Location = 373-10 Moribe, Anpachi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture | Coordinates = {{coord|35|20|37.35|N|136|41|5.70|E|scale:20000}} | Deities = [[Amaterasu]], {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime}}, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami | Shrine Rank = Shikinaisha (Small), GЕҚsha | Established = Unknown }} '''Ubanato Shrine''' (е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ) is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|е…ғдјҠеӢў|lt=Moto-Ise shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} located in Anpachi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}, representing Ubanato Shrine in Anpachi District, Mino Province. Another candidate is {{ill|Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (зҘһжҲёз”ә)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|1=Uwato Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217996}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the Rank of a GЕҚsha. {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|е…ғдјҠеӢў|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} are {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}}s that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]].{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=7pEOicqsjv0C&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA201|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai: Archaeology, History, and Mythology|date=2007-02-28|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3035-9|language=en}}{{Cite book|last=Stalker|first=Nancy K.|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=v44BEAAAQBAJ&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA62|title=Prophet Motive: Deguchi OnisaburЕҚ, Oomoto, and the Rise of New Religions in Imperial Japan|date=2007-10-31|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6404-0|language=en}} The first one was {{ill|ЕҢmiwa Shrine|en|ЕҢmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Hibara Shrine}}.{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2011-08-10|title=Hibara Jinja and Amaterasu|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2011/08/11/hibara-jinja-and-amaterasu/|access-date=2023-10-18|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}{{Cite web|title=Hibara Jinja ShrineпјҲNaraпјү|url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/en/location/290100006/?search=1&q=Hibara+Shrine|access-date=2023-10-03|website=Nationwide location database|language=en}} ==History== The founding date is unknown. According to tradition, when Princess Yamatohime-no-mikoto was searching for a suitable place to enshrine Amaterasu during the reign of Emperor Suinin, she passed through this site while traveling from {{ill|Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|ja|еӨ©зҘһзҘһзӨҫ (з‘һз©ӮеёӮ)|lt=Ikurakawa-no-miya|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|1=Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443289}} in Mino Province to Owari Province. Another theory suggests that this shrine itself was originally Ikurakawa-no-miya, supported by an inscription found on the reverse side of its treasured sacred mirror. The area historically suffered frequent flood damage. The original location of the shrine is unclear, but records indicate relocation occurred in 1603. During the Edo period, the shrine was under the protection of Owari Domain. *In 1924, the shrine relocated due to upstream construction work on the Kiso River. *In 1933, relocated again due to embankment work on the Nagara River. *In 1934, relocated to its current site due to reconstruction work on the Sai River. ==Enshrined Deities== *[[Amaterasu]] *{{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime}} *{{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} *Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami ==Transportation== *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Gikaki Line toward Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University or Gifu Distribution Center). *Approx. 2.5 km from "Minami-Omori" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Hashima Line toward Gifu-Hashima Station). *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Gifu Bus Obusa-Sunomata Line). *5-minute walk from "Moribe" bus stop (Anpachi Community Bus). ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)|version=77963473|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)]] {{wikidata link|Q11449527}} [[Category:Buildings in Anpachi Town]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Anpachi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai ShЕҚsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

During the Edo period, the shrine was under the protection of Owari Domain. *In 1924, the shrine relocated due to upstream construction work on the Kiso River. *In 1933, relocated again due to embankment work on the Nagara River. *In 1934, relocated to its current site due to reconstruction work on the Sai River. ==Enshrined Deities== *[[Amaterasu]] *{{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime|1=Toyouke-hime}} *{{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} *Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami ==Transportation== *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Gikaki Line toward Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University or Gifu Distribution Center). *Approx. 2.5 km from "Minami-Omori" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Hashima Line toward Gifu-Hashima Station). *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Gifu Bus Obusa-Sunomata Line). *5-minute walk from "Moribe" bus stop (Anpachi Community Bus). ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)|version=77963473|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)]] {{wikidata link|Q11449527}} [[Category:Buildings in Anpachi Town]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Anpachi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai ShЕҚsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}, representing Ubanato Shrine in Anpachi District, Mino Province. Another candidate is {{ill|Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (зҘһжҲёз”ә)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubanato Shrine (GЕҚdo Town)|1=Uwato Shrine (GЕҚdo-chЕҚ)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217996}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the Rank of a GЕҚsha. {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|е…ғдјҠеӢў|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} are {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}s that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]].{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=7pEOicqsjv0C&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA201|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai: Archaeology, History, and Mythology|date=2007-02-28|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3035-9|language=en}}{{Cite book|last=Stalker|first=Nancy K.|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=v44BEAAAQBAJ&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA62|title=Prophet Motive: Deguchi OnisaburЕҚ, Oomoto, and the Rise of New Religions in Imperial Japan|date=2007-10-31|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6404-0|language=en}} The first one was {{ill|ЕҢmiwa Shrine|en|ЕҢmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Hibara Shrine|1=ЕҢmiwa Shrine}}.{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2011-08-10|title=Hibara Jinja and Amaterasu|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2011/08/11/hibara-jinja-and-amaterasu/|access-date=2023-10-18|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}{{Cite web|title=Hibara Jinja ShrineпјҲNaraпјү|url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/en/location/290100006/?search=1&q=Hibara+Shrine|access-date=2023-10-03|website=Nationwide location database|language=en}} ==History== The founding date is unknown. According to tradition, when Princess Yamatohime-no-mikoto was searching for a suitable place to enshrine Amaterasu during the reign of Emperor Suinin, she passed through this site while traveling from {{ill|Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|ja|еӨ©зҘһзҘһзӨҫ (з‘һз©ӮеёӮ)|lt=Ikurakawa-no-miya|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|1=Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443289}} in Mino Province to Owari Province. Another theory suggests that this shrine itself was originally Ikurakawa-no-miya, supported by an inscription found on the reverse side of its treasured sacred mirror. The area historically suffered frequent flood damage. The original location of the shrine is unclear, but records indicate relocation occurred in 1603. During the Edo period, the shrine was under the protection of Owari Domain. *In 1924, the shrine relocated due to upstream construction work on the Kiso River. *In 1933, relocated again due to embankment work on the Nagara River. *In 1934, relocated to its current site due to reconstruction work on the Sai River. ==Enshrined Deities== *[[Amaterasu]] *{{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime|1=Toyouke-hime}} *{{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} *Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami ==Transportation== *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Gikaki Line toward Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University or Gifu Distribution Center). *Approx. 2.5 km from "Minami-Omori" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Hashima Line toward Gifu-Hashima Station). *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Gifu Bus Obusa-Sunomata Line). *5-minute walk from "Moribe" bus stop (Anpachi Community Bus). ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)|version=77963473|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)]] {{wikidata link|Q11449527}} [[Category:Buildings in Anpachi Town]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Anpachi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai ShЕҚsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

Edward|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=7pEOicqsjv0C&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA201|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai: Archaeology, History, and Mythology|date=2007-02-28|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3035-9|language=en}}{{Cite book|last=Stalker|first=Nancy K.|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=v44BEAAAQBAJ&dq=%22Moto-Ise%22&pg=PA62|title=Prophet Motive: Deguchi OnisaburЕҚ, Oomoto, and the Rise of New Religions in Imperial Japan|date=2007-10-31|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6404-0|language=en}} The first one was {{ill|ЕҢmiwa Shrine|en|ЕҢmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Hibara Shrine|1=ЕҢmiwa Shrine}}.{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2011-08-10|title=Hibara Jinja and Amaterasu|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2011/08/11/hibara-jinja-and-amaterasu/|access-date=2023-10-18|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}{{Cite web|title=Hibara Jinja ShrineпјҲNaraпјү|url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/en/location/290100006/?search=1&q=Hibara+Shrine|access-date=2023-10-03|website=Nationwide location database|language=en}} ==History== The founding date is unknown. According to tradition, when Princess Yamatohime-no-mikoto was searching for a suitable place to enshrine Amaterasu during the reign of Emperor Suinin, she passed through this site while traveling from {{ill|Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|ja|еӨ©зҘһзҘһзӨҫ (з‘һз©ӮеёӮ)|lt=Ikurakawa-no-miya|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Mizuho City)|1=Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443289}} in Mino Province to Owari Province. Another theory suggests that this shrine itself was originally Ikurakawa-no-miya, supported by an inscription found on the reverse side of its treasured sacred mirror. The area historically suffered frequent flood damage. The original location of the shrine is unclear, but records indicate relocation occurred in 1603. During the Edo period, the shrine was under the protection of Owari Domain. *In 1924, the shrine relocated due to upstream construction work on the Kiso River. *In 1933, relocated again due to embankment work on the Nagara River. *In 1934, relocated to its current site due to reconstruction work on the Sai River. ==Enshrined Deities== *[[Amaterasu]] *{{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime|1=Toyouke-hime}} *{{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} *Keitsu-Miko-no-Kami ==Transportation== *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Gikaki Line toward Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University or Gifu Distribution Center). *Approx. 2.5 km from "Minami-Omori" bus stop (Meihan Kintetsu Bus Hashima Line toward Gifu-Hashima Station). *Approx. 3 km from "Sunomata" bus stop (Gifu Bus Obusa-Sunomata Line). *5-minute walk from "Moribe" bus stop (Anpachi Community Bus). ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)|version=77963473|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:е®ҮжіўеҲҖзҘһзӨҫ (е®үе…«з”ә)]] {{wikidata link|Q11449527}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Anpachi Town]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Anpachi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai ShЕҚsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]
